title: UGC NET 2026
meta_description: UGC NET 2026 — eligibility test for Assistant Professor and JRF in Indian universities. Paper 1 & 2 pattern, syllabus, NTA notification, and preparation strategy.

hero.one_liner: National Eligibility Test for Assistant Professor positions in Indian universities and colleges + Junior Research Fellowship for PhD scholars

what_is_it: UGC NET (University Grants Commission — National Eligibility Test) is conducted by NTA (National Testing Agency) to determine eligibility for: (1) Assistant Professor positions in Indian universities, colleges, and institutions of higher education, and (2) Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) for pursuing PhD with ₹31,000/month fellowship from UGC.

The exam is conducted twice a year (typically June and December) in 83 subjects spanning Arts, Social Sciences, Sciences, Languages, and professional subjects. Each candidate takes two papers in a single 3-hour session: Paper 1 (50 questions on Teaching Aptitude, Research Methodology, etc.) and Paper 2 (100 questions on the chosen subject).

There are two levels of qualification: (1) JRF (Junior Research Fellowship) — for candidates who qualify with a higher cutoff — they are eligible for both Assistant Professor AND JRF (₹31,000/month for 2 years + SRF of ₹35,000/month for 3 years). (2) NET (Assistant Professor eligibility only) — for candidates who clear the lower cutoff — eligible to be appointed as Assistant Professor but no fellowship.

UGC NET qualification is mandatory for appointment as Assistant Professor in any UGC-recognized university or college. Without NET (or equivalent — SET/SLET, PhD with published papers under UGC 2009 regulations), you cannot be appointed permanently.

key_details.label: Conducting Body
key_details.value: NTA (National Testing Agency) on behalf of UGC

key_details.label: Education
key_details.value: Master's degree with minimum 55% marks (50% for OBC-NCL/SC/ST/PwBD/Transgender)

key_details.label: Age for JRF
key_details.value: Maximum 30 years for JRF (General). OBC: 33, SC/ST/PwBD: 35. No age limit for NET (Assistant Professor eligibility).

exam_pattern.description: Paper 1 (50 questions, 100 marks) + Paper 2 (100 questions, 200 marks) in a single 3-hour session. Total 300 marks. No negative marking.

extra_sections.heading: Paper 1 Syllabus — Common for All Subjects
content: Paper 1 tests 10 units that are common to ALL subjects:
Unit 1: Teaching Aptitude — nature, objectives, characteristics of teaching, learner characteristics, teacher characteristics, methods of teaching.
Unit 2: Research Aptitude — types of research, research methods, research ethics, thesis writing, application of ICT in research.
Unit 3: Comprehension — reading comprehension passage (3-5 questions).
Unit 4: Communication — types, barriers, effective communication in classroom.
Unit 5: Mathematical Reasoning & Aptitude — number series, data interpretation, fractions, percentages, ratio.
Unit 6: Logical Reasoning — analogies, classification, coding-decoding, Venn diagrams, syllogisms.
Unit 7: Data Interpretation — tables, charts, graphs, data sufficiency.
Unit 8: Information & Communication Technology (ICT) — basics of internet, networking, digital initiatives, e-learning.
Unit 9: People, Development & Environment — sustainable development, pollution, natural hazards, UN organizations.
Unit 10: Higher Education System — governance, UGC, AICTE, NAAC, NEP 2020, RUSA, HEFA.
Paper 1 is 100 marks with no negative marking — this is the easiest section to prepare and score well in. Many candidates focus only on Paper 2 and lose marks in Paper 1. Don't make this mistake.

extra_sections.heading: JRF Fellowship — Financial Details
content: JRF (Junior Research Fellowship) is awarded to candidates who qualify UGC NET with a higher cutoff score. JRF provides:
Years 1-2: ₹31,000/month as JRF + 20% HRA (if not provided hostel) = approximately ₹37,200/month.
Years 3-5: After 2 years, JRF is upgraded to SRF (Senior Research Fellowship) at ₹35,000/month + 20% HRA = approximately ₹42,000/month.
Total over 5 years: Approximately ₹21-22 lakh fellowship (tax-free).
Additionally: Contingency grant of ₹10,000-20,500/year for books, travel, and research expenses. House Rent Allowance if hostel not provided.

JRF is one of the best-funded PhD fellowships in India. It allows you to pursue doctoral research full-time without financial stress. Many JRF holders at central universities earn more than many entry-level corporate employees.

preparation_tips: Paper 2 is 200 marks (67% of total) — this should be your primary focus. Study your subject thoroughly using PG-level textbooks. For popular subjects like Education, Commerce, Management, Political Science, and English, coaching material and YouTube lectures are widely available.

Paper 1 is common and highly scorable. Study the 10 units from any standard UGC NET Paper 1 book (Arihant or Trueman). Higher Education System (UGC guidelines, NEP 2020, NAAC) is frequently tested and easy to learn.
No negative marking means attempt ALL 150 questions. Even if you're unsure, eliminate obviously wrong options and make an educated guess. Leaving questions blank is a pure loss.
Previous year papers (last 5-6 years) are essential. NTA often recycles concepts and question patterns. Solve all available previous papers for both Paper 1 and your Paper 2 subject.

faqs.question: I have a PhD. Do I still need NET?
faqs.answer: It depends on when you registered for PhD and under which UGC regulations. PhD holders under UGC 2009 regulations (with published research papers and coursework) are exempt from NET for Assistant Professor appointment. But PhD under older regulations may not give exemption. Many universities still prefer NET-qualified candidates. Having NET is always an advantage.

faqs.question: NET vs SET — what's the difference?
faqs.answer: NET is national-level (valid across India). SET (State Eligibility Test) is state-level (valid only in universities of that state/states that recognize it). NET qualification is superior to SET — if you clear NET, you don't need SET. If you only clear SET, you're limited to that state. Prepare for NET — if you clear it, you're covered nationally.

faqs.question: Can I teach in IITs/IIMs with UGC NET?
faqs.answer: IITs and IIMs have their own recruitment criteria which usually require PhD + published papers + significant research experience. UGC NET alone is generally not sufficient for IIT/IIM appointment, though it can be one of the eligibility criteria. NET is primarily for appointments in universities and colleges under UGC purview.

faqs.question: How difficult is UGC NET?
faqs.answer: The pass rate is approximately 6-8% overall — but this varies significantly by subject. Popular subjects like Commerce, Management, and Education have lower pass rates due to high competition. Niche subjects may have higher pass rates. With 3-6 months of dedicated preparation and thorough Paper 2 study, clearing NET is achievable for anyone with a solid Master's degree foundation.
